method: per-doc log area-ratio (first/top row median area vs all remaining rows); random-row-as-heading null; directional perm_p (frac null median-R >= S_obs); per-site + global + synthetic positive control.
key_findings: GLOBAL: S_obs=+0.048 (top-row median area ~5% larger than body), but NOT significant vs random-row null (perm_p=0.126, n=215 docs). The apparent global tilt is driven by one site.
HAGHIA TRIADA (n=128): S_obs=+0.092, perm_p=0.020, SIGNIFICANT — the top/heading row uses measurably larger glyphs than a random row would. This is the only site showing a heading-size hierarchy.
KHANIA (n=29): S_obs=-0.141, perm_p=0.977 — REVERSED: the top row is SMALLER than body rows. Strongly inconsistent with a heading-salience convention.
ZAKROS (n=20): S_obs=-0.017, perm_p=0.539 — null; no heading-size effect.
POSITIVE CONTROL PASSED: synthetic x1.7 first-row inflation detected with power=1.0 (detect_p~0.001); uniform-size docs fire at rate 0.0 (<=0.10). Machinery is well-calibrated.
VERDICT = SITE_LOCAL: the heading-size hierarchy is a Haghia Triada register, not a cross-site Linear A convention. Mirrors E081's HT-only left-margin justification pattern.
successor_hypotheses: E086: Within Haghia Triada, does the heading-size effect concentrate in a specific support type (e.g. tablets vs roundels vs nodules) or document class, rather than all HT docs uniformly?
E087: Is the HT top-row enlargement driven by a specific spatial sub-region (e.g. first row of the recto only, or a centered title band) rather than the entire top row?
E088: Test Khania's REVERSED pattern (top row smaller) — is it a bottom-row/footer enlargement, a different reading-order convention, or a corpus-composition artifact (small n=29)?

E089: Combine heading-size (this axis) with word-initial size (E077) and frequency-size (E078) per doc — do the same HT docs show multiple visual-hierarchy registers, suggesting a unified scribal salience code?
E090: Cross-check heading-size against row GLYPH COUNT (does the HT top row also have fewer/different glyphs, consistent with a title rather than a body row)?
E091: Replicate on a held-out split of HT docs to confirm the +0.092 effect is stable and not driven by a handful of outlier documents.
non_circular: Geometry only (bbox areas, row y-positions). No sign/phonetic/semantic labels. Verdict mechanically derived from frozen rule on S_obs and perm_p.
deviations: Data path: BASE/data/sigla_glyphs_bbox.json resolved to experiments/linear_a_frontier_72h/data/sigla_glyphs_bbox.json (no BASE/ dir in repo; identical file).
Positive control used ndraw=1000 (the GIVEN perm default) and 20 calibration reps for a stable false-positive estimate; GIVEN functions used verbatim, math unchanged.
